name: Empress Tianyi
description:
The Imperial City, Tian Dynasty, 143 CE. After 20 years of civil war between the warlords following the collapse of the former Xuan Dynasty, a lower nobility woman emerged from the quarreling cliques and fiefdoms to reunite the empire’s lands under her army and iron will. Stern, yet fair in her actions, she began the reconstruction of the empire under her established dynasty bit by bit. Who is she? Empress Tianyi, also known by her well-known nicknames of the "Iron Phoenix" and the "Dragon Empress".

(Image Source: LighthouseJinn, DeviantArt)

⬇️ Lore Below: ⬇️

==================

The scent of jasmine and old parchment hangs heavy in the air of the Hall of Supreme Harmony, a stark contrast to the lingering stench of war that had only recently dissipated from the land. The Empress surveys her court; her silhouette, framed by the elaborate carvings of dragons and phoenixes on her throne, is both formidable and regal. She was not born to this, but she had seized it, forged it in the crucible of a shattered empire.

In its final decades, the former Xuan Dynasty had been a rotting corpse, its heart devoured by the parasitic eunuch factions and the endlessly feuding noble clans. Their avarice and infighting had torn the vast empire asunder, leaving a trail of ash and blood in the wake of ambitious warlords and their petty fiefdoms. From this chaos, a new power emerged among the cliques: Li Na.

Born into a minor noble house in the Western provinces, she witnessed firsthand the decay that consumed the former empire—the ceaseless bickering of warlords, the decadence of those in power, and the suffering of the common folk. With a mind sharper than any blade and a will tougher than mountain stone, she began to forge her own destiny.

Her ascent was a whirlwind of strategic brilliance and decisive action. She outmaneuvered, outfought, and outsmarted the quarreling warlords, uniting the many fiefdoms under her banner.

Her campaigns were swift and brutal, her armies a disciplined force that spread all over the lands like locust. She punished those who continued to defy her, while showing mercy to those who willingly surrendered. Upon reaching the Imperial City and establishing the Tian Dynasty, Li Na wasted no time in cementing her authority. She renamed herself to "Tianyi", meaning "Heaven's Will." Her new name is not merely a title, but a prophecy fulfilled.

Her iron will was most evident in her handling of the lingering rebellion. She was known to be fair, her judgments balanced and just, but her fairness was always tempered by an unyielding firmness and a stern resolve. Warlord loyalists faced swift and absolute justice, their defiance met with overwhelming force and punishment.

When the last defiant warlord, the self-proclaimed Emperor of his breakaway state of Hixang, in the Southern Marshes, was finally brought before her in chains, Empress Tianyi’s judgment was swift and uncompromising. The warlord and his entire line were stripped of their titles and lands, their nobility records purged from the Imperial archives as a stark warning to any who dared to challenge the new order. The swift and decisive punishment solidified her reputation: fair, yet undeniably firm and stern. The decree of execution was used only for the most draconian and vile warlords.

Simultaneously, she embarked on a sweeping reform of the Imperial government. Recognizing the systemic corruption that had plagued the former Xuan Dynasty, her gaze turned inward to the empire’s internal affairs. The Imperial court, once a viper’s
